note: The shared hosted-access plan was extracted from Repixelizer into its own repo so the backbone can evolve separately from app-specific notes.

The initial first implementation hypothesis was an embedded shared gamecult_access package. This was later superseded by the Heimdall service-first shape after the cross-runtime split and Yggdrasil topology review.

StreamPixels can reuse the same auth/control-plane backbone, and Heimdall may own OAuth/token custody for shared provider connections, but streamer-audience data, creator memberships, creator-side connection bindings, and runtime state remain StreamPixels-owned.

The cross-runtime split and current Yggdrasil topology changed the first implementation shape from an embedded shared package to Heimdall: a shared auth authority service whose signed claims are verified locally by each app.

Heimdall's provider model splits shared OAuth and credential custody from app-owned binding semantics and runtime use. Different provider mixes change adapter inventory and claim vocabulary, not the core service boundary.

The first Heimdall implementation pass landed as a standalone TypeScript/Fastify service skeleton with Ed25519 JWT signing, JWKS discovery, signed OAuth state, and app-profile-driven claim issuance for Repixelizer and StreamPixels.

The first stateful Heimdall slice landed durable auth-control-plane persistence plus a real Discord callback path for Repixelizer, with accounts, linked identities, sessions, grants, entitlement snapshots, and audit events stored behind in-memory/Postgres backends.

Heimdall claim facts should prefer provider-agnostic, app-facing entitlement names such as entitlement.app_access. Provider-specific evidence like Discord role matches stays in linked identities and entitlement snapshots unless a consumer truly needs that upstream detail in the claim surface.

Browser-style Heimdall auth handoff now uses a short-lived one-time completion code: the callback page posts the result to the opener, tries to close itself, and the app backend redeems the code with Heimdall instead of receiving the final access token in a callback URL fragment.

Heimdall now supports file-backed signing-key loading/bootstrap, AES-256-GCM token sealing for managed provider credentials at rest, and a local JWT verifier seam in src/verifier.ts; the next move shifts from custody hardening to the first real Repixelizer consumer integration.

Heimdall now supports direct backend callback delivery as the preferred same-host or Yggdrasil-reachable app handoff path; the browser completion-code redeem flow remains as fallback, not the primary consumer contract.

Repixelizer has consumed Heimdall's direct backend callback flow locally, so Heimdall is no longer blocked on Repixelizer auth refactoring. The next Heimdall objective is Discord-only live launch configuration and end-to-end OAuth verification on Yggdrasil; other providers remain catalogued but callback runtimes are not implemented yet.

Repixelizer Discord launch policy is intentionally role-gated: the GameCult guild can remain public, but Heimdall should grant Repixelizer app access only to members with the Cultist role configured in GC_ACCESS_APP_REPIXELIZER_DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_IDS.

Heimdall is deployed on Yggdrasil behind nginx at https://heimdall.gamecult.org with Postgres storage, file-backed Ed25519 signing key bootstrap, configured token custody, and public JWKS/discovery/health checks passing. Repixelizer is deployed at https://repixelizer.gamecult.org in Heimdall mode with Discord as the only provider, required access enabled, and queue protection enabled; public auth-start returns a Discord authorization URL using the Heimdall callback and Repixelizer backend handoff.
Repixelizer Discord access now accepts either KLTST (861962027550244914) or the Patreon-synced Inner Sanctum role (1497585425491955894) via GC_ACCESS_APP_REPIXELIZER_DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_IDS.

Repixelizer Discord role policy moved out of Heimdall app-specific env. Repixelizer owns the GameCult guild and role list through REPIXELIZER_ACCESS_DISCORD_* and sends a caller-owned discord_role_access policy in the trusted backend-callback OAuth start; Heimdall keeps provider OAuth credentials/callback mechanics and evaluates the supplied policy.

Patreon OAuth for Repixelizer is enabled through Heimdall with API v2 identity lookup. Repixelizer owns the access rule and sends patreon_membership_access with requiredTierTitle=Inner Sanctum; Heimdall evaluates the signed-in user's currently entitled Patreon tiers by title instead of currency amount.

StreamPixels viewer and creator Twitch/YouTube OAuth now delegates to Heimdall. Heimdall owns provider OAuth, identity resolution, encrypted token custody, and app-authenticated access-token resolution; StreamPixels owns viewer profiles, creator memberships, connector bindings, diagnostics, EventSub/live-chat runtime behavior, and audience data.

Local Heimdall deployment env artifact now contains StreamPixels Twitch and YouTube OAuth client credentials under secrets/heimdall-service.env as GC_ACCESS_PROVIDER_TWITCH_* and GC_ACCESS_PROVIDER_YOUTUBE_*; values remain ignored and unprinted. Google callback URI in the downloaded secret file was ignored in favor of the configured Heimdall callback.
Twitch token exchange can return scope as an array; Heimdall now normalizes provider token scopes from string or array before storing managed credentials.

Live StreamPixels Twitch sign-in through Heimdall succeeded on Yggdrasil: Heimdall audit shows oauth_callback_succeeded, auth_completion_created, and auth_completion_redeemed for twitch after the scope normalization deploy.

Twitch EventSub subscription management must use the same Twitch application Client-ID as Heimdall creator OAuth, because Twitch authorizes EventSub creation against the app token Client-ID even when StreamPixels stores no creator refresh token.

Heimdall managed-credential resolution now refreshes Twitch and YouTube access tokens when they are expired or within the refresh window, preserving refresh-token custody in Heimdall while returning only access tokens to app runtimes.

Heimdall now issues signed app refresh tokens, exposes /v1/apps/{appSlug}/sessions/refresh, refreshes Discord/Patreon provider tokens before Repixelizer entitlement re-evaluation, and reissues short-lived app access claims without restarting provider OAuth.

Deployed Heimdall refresh-token session support to Yggdrasil and verified heimdall.service active, health/JWKS/discovery checks passing, and /v1/apps/repixelizer/sessions/refresh live with validation.

Spotiverse now consumes Spotify as a Heimdall-managed credential: Heimdall owns Spotify OAuth/token custody, and Spotiverse resolves current access via /v1/apps/spotiverse/managed-credentials/resolve without storing provider refresh tokens.

Public Heimdall can now hand Spotiverse OAuth results to a configured portal callback URL via GC_ACCESS_APP_SPOTIVERSE_BACKEND_CALLBACK_URLS; backend_callback handoffs are rejected unless the app callback is trusted, preserving token-bearing handoff boundaries.

Public Yggdrasil Heimdall was deployed with the Spotify/Spotiverse provider code, Spotify credentials from secrets/spotify.txt, a generated Spotiverse app shared secret, and GC_ACCESS_APP_SPOTIVERSE_BACKEND_CALLBACK_URLS set to the Yggdrasil loopback reverse-forward callback. Local Spotiverse now points at public Heimdall, and the Yggdrasil SSH tunnel exposes 127.0.0.1:18796 back to local Spotiverse :8796.

Added Bifrost as a Heimdall app profile for Discord/Patreon member access. Bifrost owns KTLST role and Patreon tier policies through backend-callback OAuth starts; Heimdall owns provider OAuth/token custody and generic configured backend callback allowlisting.
